Lifecycle Inversion
Daily streams as % of peak — 2025 album tracks fall in first 12 months vs The Kid I Used to Know benchmark that grew for 36 months before peaking

The catalog of 3 tracks lost ~79% of daily streams in 5 months — this isn't natural decay, it's a promo cut-off in the first 1-2 weeks after release. Minimum 1.6M streams lost vs baseline, potentially up to 5-7M lost vs a properly promoted scenario.

Why this is critical: first 6 months = 80% of a track's lifetime value. Spotify algorithm "remembers" low metrics — reanimating after 6+ months costs 3-5× more than supporting in the first month. A track that doesn't anchor in libraries within 2 weeks won't return organically.

For comparison: The Kid I Used to Know at month 13 was at 41% of its future peak — and still growing. Current tracks at that same point were already falling. This isn't a slowdown — it's inversion of a normal track lifecycle.

Catalog Health Monitor

Comprehensive scoring of all 5 key tracks based on peak retention, current daily and recovery potential

The Kid I Used to Know107.1M90.1K31.8K−65%63%🟢 92
What You're Made Of27.4M18.0K14.7K−18%58%🟢 95
Brightside24.2M66.3K8.19K−88%53%🟡 64

2025 Album — Recent Releases
Clear My Throat1.63M9.0K2.0K−78%—🟡 58
I Cry242K3.3K0.6K−82%—🔴 34
Window Pains339K1.7K0.3K−82%—🔴 28

The Kid I Used to Know — Catalog Hero. Despite −65% from peak, holds solid 31.8K daily through algorithmic radio and personal libraries. Highest ROI on promo investment — a minimal push (new trend or remix) could reignite algorithmic recommendations.

What You're Made Of — Stability Phenomenon. Only −18% from peak over 2 years — almost a miracle in the industry. Spotify algorithms "wrote in" the track for long-term recommendations. Pure passive income asset. Window open NOW for scaling.

Brightside — Classic "Flash Track" Case. Anomalous super-peak at start (66.3K daily in 2022) instantly lost 80% the next year through short viral cycle. DNA is playlist-friendly — needs external impulse to re-enter editorial lists.

Vans (King Kavalier) — Genre Fade, but TikTok-Ready. Spotify-wise depleted (−75%), BUT has 12.4K TikTok uses without any push — the cheapest path to a trend in the entire catalog. Activate before the base burns out.

Lost Streams Calculation

Daily streams lost from peak to current — sized by impact across all 5 catalog tracks

Top-3 combined loss: −119.7K daily streams (−87.4M streams over 2 years). At Spotify US rate ≈ $0.004/stream this equals $170–215K of foregone royalties — and that's only one market.

Why this is critical: catalog revenue = 60–70% of artist income. Every lost stream reduces algorithmic weight → fewer recommendations → death loop. Each week without action = streams that never come back.

Recovery Royalty Scenarios

Estimated gross royalties (US market, $0.004/stream) for different recovery levels over 24 months

ScenarioStreams/day RecoveredRoyalty/day2-Year RoyaltyArtist Net (70%)
Conservative (30%)~36K$144~$105K~$73K
Realistic (50%)~60K$240~$175K~$122K
Ambitious (70%)~84K$336~$245K~$171K
Full Peak Recovery (100%)~120K$480~$350K~$245K

This is the potential royalty income from returning a certain share of daily streams. Our task is to maximize it through streaming marketing.

Spotify vs TikTok — The Disconnect

TikTok demand exists. Spotify isn't seeing the signal because the loop is broken.

User-Generated Content

Examples of People-Generated Content with Arrested Youth Songs

▶
▶
▶
▶
▶
▶

Vans (King Kavalier Remix) — 12.4K TikTok uses vs only 13.4M total Spotify streams that have flatlined. Audience already found a use-case for the sound but algorithms don't connect TikTok demand to Spotify rotation because the artist isn't building short-form ecosystem. This is the lowest-hanging fruit in the entire catalog — ready base waiting to be ignited.

✅ The Music Already Works

Tracks accumulated 21K+ TikTok uses organically, with zero push from the artist. Audience itself found use-cases for the sound.

❌ The Artist Doesn't Exist

TikTok account effectively non-existent. Instagram unused. New releases — 28-166 uses vs 12.4K for old hits. Algorithms get no signal.

⚠️ The Trap

TikTok promotes scenarios for sound use, not songs. Without setting the format, building the trend and personal artist participation — even strong sounds stagnate.

YouTube Audit — Living in the Past

Channel powered by 2017–2019 back catalog while 2024–2025 releases collect 20–40x fewer views

🏆 TOP-5 Active Hits
The Kid I Used to Know864K
98 Degrees (Video)636K
Mirrors (Tour)567K
VANS (King Kavalier)539K
My Friends Are Robots492K
💤 TOP-5 Forgotten Hits
Little Cup (1yr)52K
Walked Out (1yr)73K
Free Now (1yr)26K
Echo (10mo)32K
Money (2yr)20K

Total YouTube lost views: −1.88M. Most painful — Find My Own Way (feat. Mark Hoppus, blink-182) got just 241K over 4 years. A collab with a legend missing ~559K potential views. No Shorts, no cross-posting, no nostalgia engine activation.

"Nostalgia engine" works organically. "The Kid I Used to Know" (2019) still pulls 864K — proof that coming-of-age content is the entry point for new audience. Shorts series in "then vs now" format would redirect old traffic to new releases.

6-Month Forecast — Three Scenarios

Catalog daily streams trajectory based on intervention level

Key insight: Playlists aren't a source of streams — they're a trigger for algorithms. Without playlists, the algorithm doesn't get enough signals → the track goes silent. With systematic playlisting of the top-3 (Kid + What You're Made Of + Brightside) the catalog grows nonlinearly. The real ceiling is not 63K daily, but 123–135K with consistent playlisting and 175K with full cross-platform activation. The 3.6x difference between worst and best case is the size of the opportunity.

Cross-Platform Problems

Five disconnected ecosystems

Spotify

Catalog passing peak → algorithm dropped tracks from editorial. No second wave of marketing support.

TikTok

12.4K organic uses on Vans, but artist account effectively absent. No farm, no trend, no UGC strategy.

Instagram

Not used. Fan-base not activated. No regular content, no UGC reposting, no engagement.

YouTube

Lost 1.88M views. No Shorts on new releases. Mark Hoppus collab wasted. Channel = archive, not living artist.

🌐
All Platforms

Releases drop into a vacuum. No regular content, no algorithm reminders, no funnel between platforms.

Reactivating Arrested Youth's Catalog

The traffic exists. The conversion system doesn't. Here's how we fix it.

Priority Actions

Six moves that change everything

#1
TikTok Activation

Official sound + 30 videos/month on artist page + TikTok farm 500+ videos/month. For 5 tracks — 2,500 videos. Vans has 12.4K ready uses waiting to be ignited.

#2
Spotify Playlists

Re-pitching and work with author playlists in US + UK + DE. The window for "What You're Made Of" is open NOW.

#3
YouTube Shorts

Reanimate Little Cup, Walked Out, Free Now through Shorts. Cinematic footage already exists — needs 15–30s vertical cuts + cross-posting to TikTok/Reels.

#4
Hoppus Collab Revival

"Find My Own Way (feat. Mark Hoppus)" — wasted asset. Shorts series "we recorded a song with blink-182" — nostalgic trigger for 25–35yo audience + social proof for global market.

#5
UGC + Nostalgia Engine

"The Kid I Used to Know" — 6 years old, still pulls 864K. "Then vs now" Shorts series to redirect old organic traffic to new releases.

#6
Release Strategy Reset

No track post-2020 crossed 100K views. Not a quality problem — a strategy problem. Next release: Shorts campaign 2-3 weeks pre-launch + official video at drop + cross-post Instagram/TikTok.

Tracks We Propose to Promote

Prioritized by recovery potential, ROI and platform readiness

🆕 2025 ALBUM — REANIMATE NOW

Clear My Throat

~1.63M total · 9K peak → 2K daily (−78%). The strongest 2025 album track — entered editorial/algorithmic playlists at start of 2026 with 1.34M streams, then dropped out. The 6-month window is closing. Re-pitch + TikTok push before the algorithm "forgets" the track.

🆕 2025 ALBUM — WEAK LAUNCH RESCUE

I Cry

~242K total · 3.3K peak → 0.6K daily (−82%). Weak start with minimal playlist support, then full fall-off. The track has not yet "frozen" in the algorithm — this is the cheapest moment to retry: fresh release window not exhausted, a single editorial pitch + TikTok could reset trajectory.

🆕 2025 ALBUM — NICHE ROTATION LOST

Window Pains

~339K total · 1.7K peak → 0.3K daily (−82%). Moderate start with niche playlist rotation, then almost complete fall-out from recommendations. Lyrical/emotional positioning fits author playlists in the indie/alt-pop space — a targeted pitch campaign could rebuild base while the release is still under 1 year old.

🔥 TOP PRIORITY #1 — POLE POSITION

What You're Made Of

Only −18% from peak over 2 years — statistically a phenomenon. Spotify algorithm wrote the track in long-term recommendations.
Window open RIGHT NOW. Pitch to momentum playlists while algorithms "remember" the track. Don't let it become the next Brightside.

⚡ TOP PRIORITY #2 — CATALOG HERO

The Kid I Used to Know

107M total streams · 63% popularity · 864K YouTube · 4.1K TikTok. Cross-platform flagship. All prerequisites for a second wave exist: external traffic via TikTok → Spotify algorithm reignites. The nostalgia engine still pulls new audience organically.

🌿 PLAYLIST-FRIENDLY DNA

Brightside

Anomalous peak of 66.3K daily in 2022 proves the playlist-friendly DNA. Needs full restart via TikTok → Spotify funnel: new viral impulse → re-entry into editorial lists. The track is dormant, not dead.

🎯 CHEAPEST WAY TO A TREND

Vans (King Kavalier Remix)

12.4K TikTok uses without any push — the only catalog asset where audience built the use-case for us. Activate immediately while the base hasn't burned out: niche club/DJ collections + UGC ignition. Lowest budget → highest ROI ratio in the catalog.

⏸️ BACKGROUND MODE

98 Degrees

Long stagnation. Internal Spotify tools "asleep" for this track. Without an external viral event (TikTok trend, sync to film/series) — reanimation = disproportionate spend. Maintain in background, watch for sync opportunities.

Core Instruments for Catalog Viralization

Four synergistic promo tools that reinforce each other

Four Pillars

📱
TikTok Content Farm

15–20 thematic accounts, 500+ videos/month → +25–50% sound usage algorithmically. TikTok ads + UGC ignition for Vans (12.4K base) and Kid (4.1K base) → Spotify & YouTube stream lift.

🎵
Spotify & YouTube Playlists

100,000+ organic streams per track via author playlists (US, UK, DE) → x3 algorithmic boost → second wind for the catalog. What You're Made Of first — window is open NOW.

📊
Marketing Strategy

Content strategy, content plan, promo strategy, social media optimization, briefs for video editors and SMM, weekly analytics dashboards.

🎬
YouTube Shorts Reactivation

Cut Little Cup, Walked Out, Free Now into 15–30s vertical Shorts. Reactivate Hoppus collab. Nostalgia engine series "then vs now" to redirect 1.88M lost view potential.

Your Growth Timeline

Pre-Launch

Days 1–14
Agreement on cooperation, promo plans and tracks (priority: What You're Made Of + Vans + Kid)
Contract signing and payment
Kick-off meeting with the team
Marketing strategy preparation
TikTok farm preparation (15–20 accounts)

Setup

Days 15–28
Marketing strategy presentation (day 15–18)
Social media optimization + content preparation
Pitching to author playlists (US, UK, DE, Western Europe) — 1 week, parallel
YouTube Shorts production from existing cinematic clips

🚀 LAUNCH

Day 28
Placement in author playlists (top-3 tracks)
TikTok farm launch (1 month run)
Shorts cross-posting begins (Instagram/YouTube)

Post-Launch

Day 28+
Interim results: 2 weeks after launch
Reporting meeting: end of month 1
Preparation for next promo stage based on MVP-test data
Focus on tracks showing strongest algorithmic response

Proposition

Distribution + Marketing — two options for New Releases and Back Catalog

Working on the future releases for Arrested Youth

OPTION 1

Distribution
+ Marketing Analysis

70/30 split
Royalty split — Artist / Distributor
Distribution
Where streams come from and how distribution impacts a release:
Editorial playlists: 30–50%  ·  Algorithmic recommendations: ~25%  ·  Direct search / social media: 25–40%

50% of a release's success is the quality of material submission, pitching text, promo plan, profile optimization and timely pitching.

Universal Music × ENKO × Artist — distribution is the difference between a track that just came out and a track that reaches up to +80% playlist-driven Spotify audience.
  • • Pitching to editorial playlists ⓘ
  • • Pitching to curator playlists ⓘ
  • • Priority Consideration via Universal Music ⓘ
  • • Real-time monitoring ⓘ
  • • Activating algorithms ⓘ
  • • Re-pitching ⓘ
  • • Personal distribution manager ⓘ
Marketing
What's included in marketing strategy:

Royalty split explanation. 70/30 means the artist keeps 70% of streaming royalties, distributor (ENKO via Universal Music) takes 30%. This split applies to both Option 1 (distribution + analysis) and Option 2 (distribution + marketing campaign). In Option 2, the additional streaming marketing campaign is paid separately by the artist.
